file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Please watch the entire video before giving an anti-coagulant shot.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: This video will teach you how to give yourself a shot of anti-coagulant medicine, also called a blood thinner.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Blood thinners help prevent clots from forming inside your blood vessels.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Before you begin, you will need alcohol wipes, your pre-filled syringe of anti-coagulant medicine,

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: and a sharp spot.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: The main parts of the syringe are the barrel containing the medicine, the plunger, the needle cap, and the needle.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Select your injection site at least two inches to the right or left of your belly button or on either of your thighs.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: A caregiver may also use these instructions to give you a shot on either side of your back, upper arms, or upper buttocks.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Use a different injection site each day to avoid swelling or bruising.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: And take your shot at the same time each day.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Be sure to follow your doctor's instructions. Do not skip any doses.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Do not inject more medicine than prescribed.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 1. Before you begin, wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 2. Clean the skin over the injection site by rubbing with an alcohol wipe in a circular motion.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 3. To open your syringe, peel the foil cover off the back.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Do not push the syringe through the foil cover because it could damage the syringe.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 4. Hold the syringe sideways by the barrel. Then, pull the needle cap straight off without bending the needle.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: There will be an air bubble in the medicine. Do not press the plunger to squirt the air bubble out of the barrel.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 5. With your free hand, pinch and hold an inch of skin at the injection site.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Insert the needle at a 90-degree angle through your skin and into the fatty tissue beneath it.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 6. Slowly press the plunger to deliver all of the medicine, including the air bubble.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 7. Pull the needle straight out of your skin.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 8. Press down firmly on the plunger to activate the safety guard which covers the needle.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Step 9. Throw away the used syringe in your sharp spots.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
text_en: Call your doctor if you notice increased bleeding or bruising, rash, numbness or tingling, especially in your legs.
